plain_message: Personal data (email, name, phone, address) should not be written to logs. Log an identifier or anonymised value instead.
Fields that store personal data must be encrypted at rest. Add encryption or use an encrypted field type before persisting.
Personal data tables should have a retention or deletion mechanism. Ensure there is a scheduled purge or soft-delete for GDPR right-to-erasure compliance.
User registration or profile creation should record marketing/processing consent. Add a consent field and timestamp if this stores personal data.
Sending personal data to a third-party service requires a Data Processing Agreement and explicit consent. Verify the recipient is GDPR-compliant.
Setting non-essential cookies without consent checking violates GDPR. Ensure this cookie is either strictly necessary or gated behind a consent check.
